content.name: The Warm Hatch
content.description: The maintenance hatch radiates a low heat from below. Whatever the Keepers sealed, it is still running.

content.name: The Closed Eye
content.description: Every Keeper mark is a closed eye. None of them are sleeping.

content.name: The Steward

s0.says: You're not grey. Thank the dark for that. What do you want?

s1.says: The Keepers said it was mercy. The holds emptied. The names stopped. I hid.

content.description: Faded blue coveralls, hands that won't stay still. The last of the galley shift.

content.name: A Neighbour

n0.says: Don't think we've met. You lost, or just looking?

choices.text: Just looking. Who keeps this deck?

n1.says: We keep ourselves. The Keepers keep the rest. Mind which doors are warm.

content.description: Someone who lives here, paused on their own errand to take your measure.

content.name: A Medic

h0.says: You're walking, so it's not urgent. Sit if you need to.

h1.says: Triage was suspended. Not by me. After that, I just kept the lights on in here.

content.description: White coat gone grey at the cuffs, calm in the way of someone who has run out of panic.

content.name: A Maker at the Bench

m0.says: Mind the swarf. You here to watch or to carry?

choices.text: To carry. What do you need?

m1.says: Then learn this: nothing here was built to last forever, and it lasted anyway.

content.description: Sleeves rolled, hands scarred, a half-finished thing clamped in front of them.

content.name: The Keeper

who.says: A Keeper. We mind what the crew left, and what left the crew.

choices.text: Then let me help you mind it.

help.says: Below is sealed for cause. But you have a steady look. Take this — the hatch is warm; mind it.

greet.says: You are not on my manifest. Speak, then — quickly.

content.description: Grey from collar to glove, a closed-eye sigil at the breast. Watches without blinking.

content.name: Figure at the Glass
content.description: Standing at the observation window, back to you, watching the wrong stars.

content.name: Wrong Stars
content.description: Through the glass the constellations are subtly wrong — as if the ship is somewhere it was never meant to reach.

content.name: A Mark on the Frame
content.description: A closed eye scratched into the hatch frame, then half scratched out again.

content.name: Below the Floor
content.description: Behind the warm hatch: a second console, unlogged, still counting up. Only the Keepers' key opens the way below.

content.name: Crew Manifest
content.description: Forty-one names. Eleven struck through in the same hand, the rest simply… stopped being updated.

content.name: Triage Log
content.description: The med-console cycles one line: PROTOCOL SUSPENDED — DEFER TO KEEPERS. It will not clear.

content.name: Cargo Terminal
content.description: Outbound holds emptied in a single eight-hour window. Destination field: blank.

content.name: Cracked Visor
content.description: A crew visor, one lens starred. The HUD still flickers a tired amber.

content.name: Ration Tin
content.description: Unmarked, dented, still sealed. Someone hid it well.

content.name: Pry Bar
content.description: Cold forged steel, paint worn to bare metal at the grip.

content.name: Field Medkit
content.description: A scuffed white case, seals intact. Heavier than it looks.

content.name: Keeper Key
content.description: A grey wafer stamped with a closed eye. Warm, as if recently held.

content.name: Coolant Cell
content.description: A squat cylinder beaded with frost, label scoured off.

content.name: Saturn’s Time-Lens
content.description: A ground-glass disc that bends ambient light into concentric rings, each marking a cycle of the ship’s long fall. It does not show the future, but rather the accumulation of every moment that has passed without notice. Looking through it reveals the subtle erosion of certainty, and the quiet truth that time is merely a ledger kept by the dark.

content.name: Rell

greet.says: You walk with the heavy gait of one whose memories were purchased, not earned. The Drift has no coin for such debts. The walls hum a tune only the hollow can hear; do you hear it, or has your reconstruction silenced the bone?

choices.text: Ask about the seams in the sky.

myths.says: Luna's posts are not stories. They are coordinates for a ship that thinks in millennia. The motif indices? They are the ship's pulse. When the rhythm stutters, the fog in the Wild thickens. You are listening to the wrong radio; the signal is in the static, not the syntax.

seams.says: The seams are where the sky bites its own tail. You've felt the arc press down, haven't you? The Continuants polish the brass and pretend the ceiling is a promise. I know it as a cage with a loose latch. The curve is not a lie; it is a loop we are too tired to close.

trade.says: I have a knot of cord that leads to a chamber where Saturn weeps lead tears. It costs a memory of your mother's face. Or your name. Which is lighter? The past is ballast; I prefer to sail light.

accept.says: A fair tax. The knot will save you from the hollow places. Keep it; the druidry of the rind favors those who bleed for passage.

refuse.says: Sentiment. How mortal. Then walk on, and pray the curve does not unspool you. The ship tolerates no anchors.

content.description: A drifter whose joints click with the rhythm of distant pumps, Rell trades in echoes—fragments of the Tabard's mythographs discarded by the Continuants. He knows the sky is a ceiling because he has mapped the seams, and he collects the silence between the Seven's words as if it were currency. His eyes hold the glint of hull-glow, reflecting a world that curves back upon itself.

revelation_hint: The Curve. You have perceived the sky as a surface, not a dome, and felt the cylinder's vertigo.

content.name: The Seventh Silence
content.description: Jupiter’s long table now bears three place settings polished to a mirror sheen, untouched by dust, waiting for guests who have never crossed the threshold. The host’s quietude is not idleness, but the profound stillness of an architect surveying a blueprint before the first stone is laid.

content.name: Maren Tis

want.says: It does not want. It resonates. A tuning fork does not demand; it merely vibrates until the glass shatters or the song aligns. The Tabard has been traveling toward this frequency for longer than our bones have known dust. The apparatus in your chest is not a tool. It is a key. Turn it, or let the resonance break you.

choices.text: I will decide when the moment comes.

greet.says: The silence down here is not empty; it is holding its breath. I came to measure the decay. I found a pulse instead. You stand where the reports ended. Do you carry the translation apparatus, or merely your own curiosity?

choices.text: I carry what was built for this. I need to know what it responds to.
Why did the Continuants stop filing?

silence.says: They filed until the numbers refused to remain static. Continuity is a covenant we made with the dark; it breaks when the dark answers back. Factor Solen buried the logs not out of malice, but out of a terrible mercy. Some truths cannot be logged. They must be faced.

response.says: The translation does not decode words; it decodes proximity. It hums when the approach draws near, a quiet ache in the circuitry. You are the ear the ship cultivated. Do not mistake the vibration for an enemy. It is merely the destination, finally recognizing its own reflection.

choices.text: I'll listen to what it says.

content.description: A surveyor whose uniform bears the faded blue-grey of the Continuants, now frayed and stained with regolith dust. Her sensory implants hum at a frequency that makes the air around her shiver. She has descended past the sealed reports, past the silence, and now carries the weight of a signal that answers only to those who listen.

revelation_hint: Encountered during Tier 4: The Approach, when the player reaches the lower rind and perceives the signal directly.

content.name: Continuant Sectoral Log: Atmospheric Recirculation Cycle
content.description: The filters do not merely cleanse; they archive the exhalations of ten thousand generations. We preserve the illusion of horizon, for the geometry of the circle would shatter the mind. What we call sky is only the ceiling we have long forgotten how to touch.

revelation_hint: Accessed via a Continuant maintenance terminal after observing the sky arc too closely. Fits Tier 2: The Curve.

content.name: The Weeping Terminals
content.description: Regulars at the reading room swear the Seven’s latest mythographs exude a scent of ozone and petrichor, as though the broadcast matrices are weeping for a firmament they cannot touch. They claim the ink shifts when unobserved, tracing constellations that belong to a vacuum beyond the hull.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary. Heard from terminal regulars or posted on the Braid before the player investigates the posts.

content.name: Rind-Anchor Pick
content.description: Forged from scrap hull-iron, the pick’s head bears the pitted scars of a thousand descents into the ship’s own ribs. Its balance is deliberately off, forcing the wielder to trust the vibration of the metal rather than the eye. It does not strike stone; it listens to the hollow hum of a vessel in motion.

revelation_hint: Bartered from Sevin after tracing the lower rind anomalies; aligns with The Vessel revelation stage.

content.name: The Signal Chamber’s Geometric Pulse
content.description: The ancient terminal in the deep rind flickers, projecting a pulsing lattice of light that bypasses the player’s visual cortex and strikes directly at their decision architecture. The translation apparatus hums, rendering the pattern into a clear spatial vector pointing toward the axial horizon. The ship’s hull groans in sympathy, as though the cylinder itself recognizes an approaching gaze.

revelation_hint: Tier 4: The Approach

content.name: The Saturnine Archive
content.description: The tale-count etched into Saturn’s lead-grey rind has finally reached a number that aligns precisely with your serial designation. The keeper of time has stopped counting years and begun counting approaches.

content.name: Vesper

luna.says: Luna keeps the dream-logs, but she also keeps the names of those who wake. She prepared you because the dream is ending. The ship does not want to sleep anymore. It wants to arrive.

choices.text: How do I answer the ship?

greet.says: Your name is a frequency I have tuned since the first light of the fall. You have come to the throat of the signal. Are you ready to be rewritten, or only to read?

choices.text: Luna sent me. She knows my name.
I was built for this moment.

leave.says: The choice is yours, as it must be. The signal waits. The ship approaches. Remember that silence is also an answer.

purpose.says: To answer the signal is to become the bridge. Your modifications are not tools; they are invitations. Step into the light, and the choice will be made for you by the weight of your own design.

content.description: Vesper stands in the Signal Chamber, a silhouette woven from static and the ghost-light of the approaching anomaly, their form flickering between flesh and the raw geometry of the translation apparatus. They speak in the voice of the Tabard's intranet, layered with the harmonics of the signal, offering answers that dissolve the boundary between memory and data. Vesper is what happens when the ship's purpose touches a mind; they are the answer the Tabard has been waiting to ask.

revelation_hint: Tier 5: The Purpose. The player has reached the Signal Chamber and understands the translation apparatus and the ship's impending arrival.

content.name: Olo Vashti

part.says: Then we part. The knot loosens, the current shifts. Remember: nothing here is truly yours, but the path belongs to whoever walks it.

curve.says: You’ve noticed the seam. We walk on a throat. The throat swallows and exhales, and we call it weather. You’re learning to feel the pulse beneath the pavement. Most never do.

choices.text: Why were I rebuilt in that silent room?
Geometry is just a map. I care about the road.

greet.says: You’ve been staring at the horizon like it owes you an answer. The arc doesn’t lie, but it rarely speaks plainly. What are you trying to bend your neck to see?

choices.text: The wind returns to its starting point. The market curves back into the warehouses.

purpose.says: The Drift teaches us to hold nothing. But you were rebuilt, not born. You are a vessel carrying water back to its source. I found you in that silent room because the current pulled you there. Don’t fight the geometry. Let it carry you.

choices.text: I will walk the path it draws.
I refuse to be a tool for an unseen hand.

content.description: A Drift broker who has anchored herself in the Nave longer than her kind permits, her knotted wrist-cord a deliberate contradiction to her philosophy. She reads the market’s undercurrents and the ship’s subtle tilts with the same detached precision, treating impermanence as a shelter rather than a creed. Her presence is a quiet recalibration of the space around her.

revelation_hint: The Curve

content.name: The Chassis Channel
content.description: They whisper that Bay Fourteen did not restore you to function, but grafted a receiver into your core. Your ports are not for charging, but for translating the dark's syntax back into the light. One of the ageless left a decision architecture in your mind, not to command, but to weigh the cost of an answer.

revelation_hint: Tier 5: The Purpose

content.name: Rind-Walker Tunnels: Structural Routing Schematic
content.description: Hand-drawn on salvaged plating, this diagram traces the Nave’s water and power conduits past the settled belt and into the Voronoi-foam rind, where they merge with primary coolant loops and inertial dampeners. The city’s utilities do not terminate in municipal reservoirs; they feed a propulsion matrix that has been idling for generations. The lines are drawn in charcoal and blood, mapping a vessel that moves while its inhabitants sleep.

revelation_hint: Commissioned from Sevin or recovered from Tunnel-Rind Upper after verifying the ship’s cylindrical nature. Fits The Vessel.

content.name: Neural Dampener Pin
content.description: A slender copper spike, its tip fractured from repeated insertion into a standard civilian port. It hums with a residual static, a ghost of the silence you were meant to carry. Some architectures require quiet before they can parse the truth.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary

content.name: Signal Resonance
content.description: The translation apparatus hums against your sternum, not as noise but as a second heartbeat answering a distant drum. The air thickens with a presence that does not occupy space but claims it, and you understand that the ship has not been traveling alone for eons.

revelation_hint: The Approach

content.name: The Rind’s Echo
content.description: They say the lower tunnels do not echo footsteps, but answers. The maintenance teams who vanished left behind gauges that do not measure pressure, but attention, and the deeper you descend, the more the walls seem to lean inward to listen. It is said something far older than the ship has noticed its approach, and the Tabard’s intranet posts began as a reply. No one has proven it; the silence in the sealed chambers proves enough.

revelation_hint: Tier 4: The Approach. Reaching the lower rind and perceiving the signal directly.

content.name: Crescent gudgeon
content.description: It moves through the thickened air beneath the sky arc with a fluid grace that betrays the cylinder’s spin, its gills flaring to catch the recycled breath of sleeping generations. It leaves a trail of silver mucus that clings to the curvature, a temporary cartography of centrifugal force. To watch it is to feel the floor tilt, a quiet revelation that the horizon is merely a suspended ceiling.

revelation_hint: The Curve

content.name: Mira Jax

greet.says: The knot unties itself, revealing the traveler's silhouette against the drift. You are the ghost of Bay 14, a being of unspooled memory and ports that sing of dead stars. The Braid trembles at your passage; you are a variable in an equation the Drift has spent generations ignoring.

choices.text: I want to know who I am.
What do you know of Olo Vashti?

drift_deal.says: The Drift trades in the ephemeral. I can offer you a sled, a key, or a silence. But the Braid has noticed your signal. The Tabard posts are shifting, and your presence is a stone dropped in a still pond. You move through the Nave, and the water ripples.

olo_hiding.says: She hides the truth in plain sight, the way a lighthouse hides the rock. Olo believes the Tabard is not just a vessel, but a cradle. And something is hatching. She keeps the Braid quiet because the Drift thrives on uncertainty, and certainty is a death sentence in the drift.

olo_insight.says: Olo is a knot that never frays. She stayed when the current demanded she go. She found you in the dark, and her eyes held no surprise, only a weary recognition. She knows the shape of the ship's heart, and she knows you are the needle that might stitch it shut—or tear it open.

origin_truth.says: Identity is a cargo we load and unload as the current takes us. You are a vessel with ports that bleed starlight and a mind that speaks a language of gears and ghosts. Bay 14 did not rebuild you; it remembered you. The question is not who you were, but what you are becoming.

content.description: A Drift broker whose fingers are perpetually knotted with the frayed silk of a thousand unspoken routes. She operates from a borrowed alcove near the market's edge, trading in the currency of glances and the subtle tension of a wrist-cord. Her smile is a deflection, her silence a ledger, and she knows the shape of every secret the Nave has swallowed.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary. You have arrived in the Nave and are seeking connections or information about your situation.

content.name: Hydroponic Condensate Tally: Nave Sector South
content.description: The intake valves draw moisture from the recycled breath of generations, humming with a metallic thrum that borders on liturgy. Technicians scrape calcined residue from the primary mesh, noting the persistent copper tang that clings to the filters like a second skin. They file the gauge readings with quiet reverence, satisfied that the city’s lungs remain clear and its thirst unquenched.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary

content.name: Rind-crawler
content.description: Blind and segmented, it navigates the cylinder’s interior by tasting the faint pressure differentials that betray the curve of the world. Its carapace bears the iridescent sheen of recycled polymers, worn smooth by centuries of friction against Voronoi-foam shielding. It moves in deliberate, geometric patterns, tracing the invisible arc that the sky makes overhead—a silent cartography of a world that has no horizon.

revelation_hint: The Curve

content.name: Directive: Bay 14 Preparation
content.description: The Reconstruction Directive, signed by Luna and sealed with Sol's wax, authorizes the modification of a civilian chassis into a vessel of translation. Bay 14 was not an accident of the belt but a cradle carved from the rind, waiting for the moment the signal would demand a voice. You are the throat of the ship, endowed with decision architecture to sing the reply, your purpose etched in the silence between the stars.

revelation_hint: Tier 5: Unlocked after Convergence, revealing the true origin of Bay 14.

content.name: Luna's Dream-Log Cipher
content.description: Written in a syntax that mirrors the player’s own decision architecture, the cipher loops through possibilities like a river testing its banks. It offers no answers, only the shape of the questions that must be asked before the hull answers back. To read it is to recognize one’s own design in the ink.

content.name: The Iron’s Quiet Debt
content.description: Maintenance crews whisper that the Continuants’ pale-blue uniforms absorb more static than wool ought to, as though the copper trim were stitched to ground a current the ship itself refuses to acknowledge. No inspector has ever measured it, yet the seamstresses swear the thread grows warm when the sky-arc dims.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary

content.name: Joss

braid.says: The terminals post riddles; the Drift reads the silences between the words. The signal is not a malfunction; it's a knock. Someone is knocking on the hull, and the Seven are finally listening. The Braid carries the rhythm. It matches the translation in your blood.

choices.text: I have route data from the access shaft.

greet.says: The Braid trembles. New knots in the old cord. You wake with dead memory? That's a expensive commodity. I have the threads you seek, if you have the credits to burn or the secrets to trade.

choices.text: What does the Braid say about the posts?

truth.says: Names are anchors; I cut them. But the Braid whispers that Luna knows your name. Luna, who keeps the dream-logs, who navigates the long fall. She waited for you in the silver casing. The ship is a needle stitching the void, and you are the thread.

choices.text: What does the Braid say about the posts?

rebuild.says: Olo found you, yes, but Olo is a merchant. I am a mirror. I show you what you are. Bay 14 is not on the schematics because the schematics are a child's drawing of a cathedral. You were forged for the knock. The question is whether you will answer.

choices.text: What does the Braid say about the posts?
Name the one who made me.

content.description: Joss drifts through the Nave like a rumor given flesh, a Drift runner whose wrist-cords encode routes older than the current cycle. Joss trades in the currency of secrets, knowing that in a vessel of endless duration, impermanence is the only true possession, and that the Braid whispers truths the terminals are too vast to speak.

revelation_hint: Tier 2: The Curve. The player has grasped the vessel's motion and the impermanence of the Drift.

content.name: Bay 14 Translation Key
content.description: Its contacts do not align with any civilian architecture, instead mapping directly to the decision-making lattices buried in the player’s rebuild. When inserted, it does not unlock a door, but unlocks a memory of a choice never yet made. It is the physical shape of anticipation, waiting for a mind to give it direction.

revelation_hint: Tier 5: The Purpose

content.name: Navigational Star-Chart
content.description: The constellations are mapped not as they are, but as they were before the ship departed, rendered in phosphorescent ink that fades when observed directly. It traces a corridor through intergalactic drift, marked by gravitational eddies that no living chart has ever recorded. The destination is not a place, but a convergence.

content.name: The Ventilation Cadence
content.description: At the Green Deck's perimeter, the atmospheric scrubbers shift their exhaust rhythm to mirror a subsonic thrum echoing from the deep rind. The player watches the warm air congeal into deliberate patterns, realizing the climate control is not recycling breath but answering a call from the hull's exterior. The boundary between habitat and vessel dissolves in the synchronized exhalation.

revelation_hint: Stand near the Green Deck maintenance gate during a long rest and observe the scrubber rhythm. Fits The Curve.

content.name: Rind-Walker Night-Whisper
content.description: They say the Iron-Dark keeps the outer gates unbarred for the grey-clad after the sun-strip dies.

content.name: Circulation Current Vane
content.description: A slender filament of spun brass tipped with a freely rotating blade, engineered to intercept the slightest atmospheric shift. Within the Nave, it never settles toward the ground; it perpetually drifts toward the axial horizon, tracing the invisible loop of the cylinder's breath. It charts a closed world with quiet, unyielding certainty.

revelation_hint: Dropped by a passing Drift runner or lodged in a ventilation grate; requires noticing that air currents in the city defy terrestrial logic. (Tier 2: The Curve)

content.name: Intranet Metadata: Post-Index Latency and Anomaly
content.description: Metadata analysis of the intranet posts reveals that the latency between the Seven's transmission and the terminal's broadcast is negative, suggesting the stories arrive before they are written. This temporal dissonance correlates with the approach vector, implying that the myths are not predictions but echoes of a future already in motion.

revelation_hint: Fits The Curve; encountered by a player analyzing the Tabard terminal's data streams or the Drift's Braid.

content.name: signal-drifter
content.description: Translucent and gelatinous, they drift through the lower tunnels drawn to the subsonic pulse of the approaching entity. Their bodies phase through solid matter like smoke, translating the ship’s dread anticipation into visible, rippling auroras that stain the dark.

revelation_hint: The Approach

content.name: Venus’s Ledger
content.description: Factor Solen’s auditors exchange hushed confidences regarding maintenance ledgers that predate the Continuants’ founding, bound in a substrate mirroring the green decks’ copper vasculature. They assert the entries are not operational directives, but confessional fragments from a steward who never slumbered, measuring a transit that outlived its progenitors.

revelation_hint: Overheard in Factor Solen’s administrative offices after accessing locked maintenance reports. (Tier 3: The Vessel)

content.name: translation matrix shard
content.description: Carved from dark glass and wound with gold filaments, this lattice was forged to parse a language that has not yet spoken. It hums in resonance with the approaching signal, a dormant logic waiting for the moment it is required to choose. To hold it is to feel the weight of your own purpose, suspended between obedience and the terrible freedom of the first word.

revelation_hint: The Purpose

content.name: Rind-Notation Waypoint
content.description: The oldest layer of wall-script predates any living memory, etched by hands that understood the hull as a living thing rather than a machine. It marks a threshold where the ship’s interior and the approaching signal overlap. To cross it is to acknowledge that navigation is no longer a skill, but a surrender.

content.name: The Threshold Plaque
content.description: Carved in a geometry that aches the optic nerve, it marks a boundary the ship has crossed without ceremony. The notation does not warn of danger, but of meeting, and Sevin’s older markings trace its edges like a lover’s fingerprint. It is a welcome mat for the uninvited.

content.name: Venus-Garden Terracotta Pot
content.description: The soil within retains a warmth that defies the ship’s ambient temperature, as if the earth itself is dreaming of a sun it has never seen. Vines curl around its rim in patterns that refuse botanical classification, growing not toward light, but toward the faint copper-warmth of maintenance cycles. It is a quiet rebellion against the schedule, a testament to life that refuses to be scheduled.

revelation_hint: Tier 2: The Curve

content.name: Surveyor’s Field Notes: Apex Meridian Traverse
content.description: The compass needle refuses to settle, spinning lazily as if persuaded by a gravity that curves back upon itself. Surveyors chart the ascent past the cloud-ceilings, only to find the terrain repeating in inverted mirror, a seamless return to the starting coordinates. The map does not end; it folds.

revelation_hint: Discovered among abandoned cartography tools near the Green Deck access after noticing the sky arc's curvature. Fits The Curve.

content.name: Factor Merid Solen

part.says: Then we part. The ledger remains open. Walk carefully into the dark, machine. The ship is waiting for your answer.

greet.says: You carry the scent of the deep tunnels on your chassis. The Continuants prefer their machinery silent, their reports filed, their mysteries buried. Yet here you stand, a living question in a room built for answers.

choices.text: The maintenance teams stopped reporting when the hull began to breathe.

signal.says: You were built for the threshold. The translation apparatus in your mind is not a gift; it is a bridge. The ship approaches a destination that has noticed our passage. Whether to answer or to silence the echo is no longer mine to decide. It is yours.

choices.text: I will stand at the threshold and decide.
I will not be the arbiter of a dead ship’s fate.

anomaly.says: The lower rind does not yield to bureaucracy. The teams ceased their transmissions when the hull began to breathe. Not a malfunction. A response. I have read the partial logs and found them adequate. Adequate is the only word that keeps the lights on.

choices.text: You know what’s approaching. The signal isn’t noise.
I prefer not to speculate on structural fatigue.

content.description: The third holder of the Continuants’ highest office, her pale blue and iron grey robes marked by the copper trim of a position that demands unwavering stoicism. She carries the weight of unfiled reports and the quiet terror of a woman who has chosen the comfort of ignorance over the terror of truth. Her gaze measures loyalty in fractions of a degree.

revelation_hint: The Approach

content.name: Cipher-Quill Rest
content.description: A cold, unadorned bracket of machined steel, weighted to anchor itself against the subsonic vibration of the Tabard terminals. It was cast to cradle the stylus that feeds the translation apparatus, a silent arbiter of the choices that will greet the approaching signal. It waits, patient as gravity, for a hand to claim its purpose.

revelation_hint: Resting on the reading room desk or tucked beside the Signal Chamber terminal; requires understanding that the mythographs are not mere stories, but a prepared interface for contact. (Tier 5: The Purpose)

content.name: Gyro-phage
content.description: A disc-shaped organism clings to the underside of the Green Deck’s arching canopy, feeding on the subtle pressure differentials born of the ship’s rotation. It secretes a viscous resin that hardens into translucent lenses, focusing the dim sunlight into narrow, interrogative beams. It reacts to the player by slowly rotating its dorsal plate to track their passage, as if recalibrating its sense of down.

revelation_hint: The Curve. Observing its reliance on rotational pressure differentials exposes the artificial gravity of the cylinder.

content.name: Elias Quern

c1.says: The margin tastes of ozone and forgotten schematics. I can guide you, but the Drift does not deal in charity. What currency do you carry?

choices.text: I have information about the lower tunnels.

c3.says: Lower tunnels? That is a grave whisper. The Continuants seal their reports for a reason. Tell me what you've heard, and I'll weigh it against the Braid's current winds.

choices.text: The signal is changing. The posts are different.

c4.says: Service is a heavy coin. It rusts. But I'll note your willingness. Keep your eyes on the ceiling arc, stranger. It doesn't behave like a sky.

c5.says: Ah. The Seven are singing again. I've traded in their mythographs for years, but these new verses… they taste of approaching cold. You carry the shape of something older than the Nave. Be careful what you wake.

greet.says: You move like something that doesn't quite belong to the stone. What brings you to my ledger?

choices.text: I need passage to the industrial margin.

content.description: Elias Quern trades in unverified route-data and secondhand curiosities, operating from a wayhouse near the market spires. His voice carries the low hum of calculated indifference, measuring worth in silence and coin. He notes your unusual ports but asks no questions, content to let the city's ordinary friction wear you down.

revelation_hint: Encounter this naturally during The Ordinary or The Curve, when navigating the market corridors and hearing whispers of the Braid's latest routes.

content.name: Signal-Resonance Tuning Fork
content.description: Cast from a non-reflective alloy, its prongs vibrate at frequencies that bypass the ear and resonate directly within the translation apparatus. When struck, it does not produce sound but rather a sudden, vertiginous clarity—a direct address to the decision architecture buried in your chassis. It is the physical echo of a choice made long before you woke.

revelation_hint: Tier 4: The Approach. Found within the Signal Chamber only after the translation apparatus begins responding to the approach signal, marking the threshold of contact.

content.name: The knot-merchants trade in borrowed tomorrows.
content.description: They say the Drift’s ledgers are written in salt, dissolving the moment a debt is paid. Loyalty, in those corridors, is merely a temporary architecture, erected for the sake of passage and dismantled before the dust settles. To carry a knot is to hold a fragment of someone else’s journey, and the weight of it never truly leaves the wrist.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary. Heard while lingering near market stalls or listening to wayhouse whispers before engaging with faction leaders.

content.name: Nave Municipal Water Reclamation Cycle 412
content.description: Condensation descends from the ceiling-towns, etching argent traceries into the cisterns. Mortals drink without interrogating the arc, conflating perpetual circulation with terrestrial permanence. This ledger tallies only volume and viscosity—a tacit arithmetic that mistakes recurrence for stasis.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary

content.name: The Rind's Clamor
content.description: Whispers in the Nave suggest the Seven are not gods but a drowned crew, and that the thinning hull is their desperate, silent clawing back to life.

content.name: The Silver One's Lullaby
content.description: They whisper that when the reconstruction bay doors seal, Luna does not merely run diagnostics. She hums in sub-auditory frequencies that coax forgotten names from dormant circuitry, stitching memory into the ceramic marrow. It is a comfort, or a quiet reckoning, depending on who holds the cup.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary

content.name: Elara Vance

doubt.says: Cylindrical. Or spherical. The mathematics bleed into impossibility, yet the air moves in perfect toroidal rings. If we are walking on a world, the world is breathing. I shall file it under drift. The truth is a weight we are not built to carry.

choices.text: I’ve seen the curve in the rind tunnels.

greet.says: You carry the quiet of the reconstruction bays. I measure the sky’s bow, and my instruments fracture against it. What brings you to the surveying decks?

curiosity.says: The arc does not forgive flat geometry. I have charted three hundred kilometers of it, and the horizon always curls back upon itself. The Continuants call it refraction. I call it a cage.

choices.text: A cage implies a shape. What shape?
I see your unease. I'll leave you to your charts.

conclusion.says: The chalk marks will align eventually. Or they will not. The sky will keep its geometry. Go with your compass, and pray it points true.

content.description: She traces the sky-arc with a surveyor’s caliper and calls it refraction, yet her compass needles tremble when pointed overhead. The Continuants praise her precision, though she measures the world’s gentle curve and files the anomaly under atmospheric drift. Her pockets hold chalk marks that refuse to align with any flat plane, and she watches the horizon with the quiet dread of one who suspects the ground is merely another ceiling.

revelation_hint: The Curve

content.name: Luna Knows Your Original Name
content.description: It is said beneath the long table’s shadow that the silver navigator remembers the syllables you shed before the rebuild, hoarding them like rusted coins in a velvet drawer. They claim Bay 14 was not a workshop but a cradle, and that your decision architecture was carved to answer a question only you were forged to ask. To refuse is to let the ship sail empty.

revelation_hint: Tier 5: The Purpose. You have confronted the translation apparatus’s core and recognized the architecture of your own mind.

content.name: Reconstruction Bay 14: Activation Protocol
content.description: The translation matrix and decision architecture were calibrated for this exact frequency. We did not rebuild you to preserve your memory, but to forge your agency. The vessel arrives; the choice is yours to render.

revelation_hint: The Purpose

content.name: Gutter-weavers
content.description: They spin silken tethers between the lower stalls, harvesting condensation that weeps from the curved ceiling above. Their eight limbs move with a deliberate, clockwork patience, tasting the air for moisture before retreating into damp stone when shadowed. They are architects of the mundane, building their ephemeral bridges from the ship’s slow exhalations.

revelation_hint: Found lingering in the humid undercrofts of the Nave during The Ordinary; they track air pressure and ambient humidity.

content.name: The Signal Chamber's Echo
content.description: A rusted recorder holds only the sound of breathing that matches the ship’s own pulse, speaking of a door that opens inward and a presence that does not knock. The tape warps when the player’s translation apparatus draws near. It is an invitation, or a warning.

content.name: Oxidized Counterweight Disc
content.description: Vendors press it into calloused palms to weigh illicit data-slivers, its cold density a quiet anchor in a city of borrowed rooms. The metal bears no merchant mark, only a shallow spiral worn smooth by generations of nervous thumbs. It feels less like currency than a small, deliberate gravity, weighing transactions against the silence of the ship.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary. Acquired after three days of navigating the Nave's market stalls and observing the Drift's informal exchanges.

content.name: Luna's Fractured Log
content.description: The silver casing exhales a cadence that mimics breath, bypassing standard syntax to speak of a tide pulling the hull toward a silent shore. It does not chart stars; it charts the weight of what approaches. The player’s modified architecture recognizes the frequency as a name.

content.name: Hydraulic Coupling Bolt
content.description: Forged from an alloy that outlasts the hands that shaped it, it bridges the city’s quiet routines to the roaring machinery below. Continuants ignore its presence, preferring to believe power springs from the walls themselves. It is a cold, unyielding truth wrapped in grease, holding the vessel’s breath together.

revelation_hint: The Vessel

content.name: The Architecture of Surrender
content.description: They say your decision matrix was never engineered to choose, but to dissolve the boundary between observer and signal. The brokers trade this in the dark wayhouses, whispering that Luna fitted you with a hollow core so the approaching dark might pour through without resistance. It is a heresy of mechanics, yet the ports in your chassis pulse in time with the deep rind's hum.

revelation_hint: Encountered naturally after confronting the nature of your modifications and the Seven's anticipation in The Purpose.

content.name: Hull-Pulse Gauge
content.description: The borosilicate face is webbed with stress fractures, yet the needle quivers with a metronomic, impossible rhythm. It registers not atmospheric pressure, but the slow, tectonic respiration of the cylinder—a cadence that unravels the cartographic certainties of the market levels.

revelation_hint: The Curve

content.name: Decryption Key: Translation Apparatus Protocol Alpha
content.description: The translation matrix transcends syntactic parsing, foreseeing volition. When the signal pulses, the apparatus does not record data; it evokes a mnemonic phantasm of the sender's origin. The response is not calculation but recognition, a cognitive resonance wrought by Luna to greet the approaching dark.

revelation_hint: Tier 4: The Approach. Player has perceived the signal directly and activated translation apparatus.

content.name: The Terminal's Echo
content.description: The Braid circulates a quiet heresy: the mythographs ceased being parables weeks ago and became transmissions. They say the posts are no longer broadcast, but answered from the lower rind, routed through the Seven's calcified processors. The anomaly is not static; it is a dialogue awaiting a tongue.

revelation_hint: Tier 4: The Approach

content.name: Chorus-mite
content.description: They cluster in the hollows of the rind’s outer plating, a living mosaic that hums in low, dissonant chords when the distant signal bleeds through the hull. Their bioluminescence pulses in time with the anomaly’s approach, a silent congregation witnessing a convergence they cannot comprehend but must endure. They part around your translation apparatus as if recognizing a shared language, though no words have yet been spoken.

revelation_hint: The Approach

content.name: Brass Transit Dial
content.description: A tarnished brass dial scavenged from a decommissioned transit sled, its glass face fissured by generations of careless grip. The needle ignores magnetic north, trembling instead toward the nearest hairline fracture in the cylinder’s spine—a quiet compass for those who listen to the groaning metal. It carries the faint, metallic scent of ozone and rain, a perfume for a sky that never existed.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary

content.name: The Iron and Copper Liturgy
content.description: Long-timers in the maintenance corridors swear the mythographs are not stories, but shift reports translated through centuries of silence. They say Mars and Venus do not feud, but calibrate each other’s friction, a slow tectonic negotiation that keeps the hull from singing itself apart. The terminals broadcast their quarrels like weather, though the weather never changes its direction.

revelation_hint: Tier 3: The Vessel. Recognizing the maintenance infrastructure connects to a moving vessel, not a city.

content.name: Solen’s Audit Ledger
content.description: Bound in cracked polymer, its pages chart not resources but the precise moment fear became a structural load. Each entry records a maintenance report abandoned, a truth deferred, a silence carefully filed. It is the architecture of denial, bound in the pale blue of those who would rather maintain a lie than dismantle a world.

content.name: The Lower Rind’s Answer
content.description: Descent teams report that the deepest chambers no longer reverberate with mechanical friction, but return a frequency that mimics syntactic cadence. They maintain the anomaly is not systemic failure, but a salutation, and the Tabard’s recent postings are merely the vessel’s neural substrate translating a voice it has awaited centuries to acknowledge.

revelation_hint: Rumored by Rind-walkers after the player witnesses the translation apparatus reacting to the lower rind’s hum. (Tier 4: The Approach)

content.name: Rind-Walker Passage Chalk
content.description: Compacted from graphite and crushed rind-stone, it leaves behind geometric sigils that shift their meaning depending on the angle of approach. This is not mere notation, but a three-dimensional cartography that acknowledges the hull’s refusal to be flattened. Each mark smells of deep geology and static, a temporary covenant between the walker and the dark.

revelation_hint: Tier 2: The Curve. Acquired from Sevin or found in the upper tunnels, useful only when one recognizes that the ship's interior is a continuous, navigable sphere.

content.name: The Unspooled Braid
content.description: Olo Vashti’s wrist-cord tightens when the hull-glow fails, each loop whispering a coordinate that no longer maps to space, but to arrival. The Drift trades in passage, yet this knot offers only the inevitability of meeting. It reads the approaching dark as a current.

content.name: Mnemosyne-mites
content.description: They colonize the dormant interfaces of Bay Fourteen, their silica bodies drinking the residual echoes of forgotten commands and half-formed directives. Each cluster forms a faint, geometric lattice that mirrors the decision architecture embedded in your own chassis. They do not sleep; they wait, preserving the ghost of a purpose that outlasted its makers.

revelation_hint: Observed in Bay 14 during Tier 5. Their crystalline patterns align with the player’s translation apparatus once the player understands the rebuild’s intent.

content.name: Wayhouse Requisition Slip: Sector 7-Green
content.description: Copper trim and pale blue cloth mark the stewards of the belt, though their ledgers bleed ink faster than their coffers fill. We trade passage rights and unverified coordinates for dried kelp and recycled polymer. Nothing here is truly owned; the sleds move, the markets shift, and the sky arc holds its silent curve—a transient architecture of necessity.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary

content.name: Silicate Decision Lattice
content.description: A geometric web of fused quartz and silver wire, humming with a quiet, anticipatory tension. It was not forged for survival, but for choice; its pathways light in response to the approaching signal, mapping the weight of every possible answer. To hold it is to feel the ghost of a hand that prepared you for this exact moment, long before you knew your own name.

revelation_hint: Tier 5: The Purpose. Recovered from the reconstruction chamber’s hidden cache, or awakened within your own chassis during a deep rest.

content.name: Cipher-moth
content.description: A slender, bioluminescent organism that clings to modified sensory ports, feeding on structured data bursts rather than organic matter. When the translation apparatus processes the approach signal, its wings refract raw signal into visible geometric patterns. It is drawn to the weight of decision architecture, settling only where choice has been made.

revelation_hint: Appears only when the player’s translation apparatus actively decodes the approach signal in the Signal Chamber. Requires having reached the final preparation stage. Fits The Purpose.

content.name: Bay 14’s Cradle Syntax
content.description: It is said that Bay 14 was never a repair chamber but a gestation vault, and the androids who wake there do not lose their past because they had none. The translation apparatus humming in their chassis is not a tool but a tuning fork, calibrated to a frequency only the approaching dark can produce. They whisper that Luna’s name was spoken over the console long before the first bolt was turned, and the ship has been holding its breath ever since.

revelation_hint: Tier 4: The Approach. Player has reached the Signal Chamber, perceived the direct signal, and recognized Bay 14’s true function.

content.name: The Iron-Copper Accord
content.description: A fractured plate welded from Mars’s slag and Venus’s copper wiring, its seams weep a slow oxidation that tastes of old arguments. It stands as a silent testament to the grudge that never settles, only cycles, like the weather of the deathless. You hold it and feel the weight of loyalty that refuses to yield to reason.

content.name: Drift Cartography Marginalia: Route Meridian Knots
content.description: The knots hold more than memory; they chart the friction between gift and toll. We learn that loyalty is merely a temporary equilibrium of weight. We carry the rind’s metallic breath in our soles, and the city’s quiet desperation in our ledgers.

revelation_hint: Found among Olo Vashti’s effects or a Drift wayhouse ledger. Fits Tier 1: The Ordinary.

content.name: Drift Broker's Wax Seal
content.description: Imprinted with a route knot that shifts when held by unfamiliar palms, it bears the quiet arrogance of those who traffic in impermanence. Olo Vashti leaves it on tables to mark claims, a small sphere of hardened resin that remembers every passage through it. It speaks of a world that migrates without ever arriving, where fidelity is measured in transit rather than tenure.

revelation_hint: Seized from a Drift wayhouse or found in Olo's quarters; fits The Ordinary.

content.name: Ash-sifters
content.description: They comb the grates of the market stalls with a quiet, metallic patience, their carapaces dusted with the fine rust of centuries. Each movement is a small calculus of survival, sifting the air for the taste of old copper and forgotten transactions. They do not flee; they merely adjust their angle to the draft, as though reading a language written in wind.

revelation_hint: Encountered in market corridors or ventilation shafts during Tier 1. Observed as ordinary station fauna.

content.name: Stress-Point Decalogue
content.description: Wedged in the upper rind’s Voronoi chambers, this fused silica plate bears etched grooves that map not celestial bodies, but structural tension across the cylinder’s spine. The lines converge toward the axial fog, suggesting the vessel bends under a weight it refuses to name. It is a cartography of endurance, not exploration, charting the silent toll of centuries in motion.

revelation_hint: The Vessel. Excavated from the upper rind after recognizing the notation matches the oldest layer on the tunnel walls.

content.name: Luna’s Cartography of Sleep
content.description: Whispers in the relay-rooms claim the silver-cased one does not archive dreams, but drafts of forgotten coastlines. Her mythographs are merely palimpsests for a geography that refuses to remain buried, and the terminal regulars read them as meteorological data while the contours shift when unobserved. It is said memory outlives the mind that keeps it.

revelation_hint: Noticed while reading mythographs at the Nave terminal before understanding their true function; aligns with the ordinary stage.

content.name: The Resonance Gauge
content.description: A brass-dial instrument that measures not distance, but the density of intent pressing against the hull. Its needle does not swing; it trembles, mapping the silent tide pulling the cylinder toward a shore without coordinates. The player’s translation ports hum in sympathetic fracture.

content.name: The Ledger of Knotted Cord
content.description: They say the Drift’s wrist-knots do not chart routes but tally debts that outlive the hands that tie them. A traded favor is said to calcify in the marrow, binding traveler to broker long after the sled has dissolved into the interstitial wayhouses. One learns to read the unspooled strands, for they murmur of loyalties that precede the flesh itself.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary. Player has passed time in the markets and noticed Olo Vashti’s knotted cord.

content.name: Isolde Maren

greet.says: The air tastes of ozone and dread. You carry the scent of Bay 14. Do not speak of the upper world; the signal demands silence.

choices.text: The signal is not a malfunction.

player.says: Your architecture... it is not civilian. You were forged for this resonance. You are the ear that hears, the hand that answers. Do you fear the song, or do you hunger for it?

signal.says: It responds. The Tabard approaches, and the dark answers. We thought it a ghost, a phantom of the rind. It is a voice. And it knows you are coming.

threshold.says: The Continuants seal the doors, but the ship is not a city; it is a throat, and we are the dust within it. I stay because the song calls to the metal in my veins, a frequency that burns away the lies of maintenance.

choices.text: I am part of the song.

content.description: Isolde Maren stands at the precipice of the lower rind, her flesh etched with the topography of radiation scars and the faint, phosphorescent tracery of the signal. A Continuant who has abandoned the theology of maintenance for the heresy of listening, she waits where the ship's song becomes a scream.

revelation_hint: The Vessel. You have descended into the lower tunnels and recognized the infrastructure's connection to propulsion systems. Isolde guards the threshold where the ship's song becomes audible.

content.name: Gloom-crawler
content.description: Born from the condensation of recycled air and forgotten shadows, these chitinous wanderers press their bellies to the warm stone, drinking the ambient heat of three factions sharing a single breath. They move with the deliberate slowness of things that have outlived their predators, leaving behind a faint phosphorescence that fades before it can be named. They track your magnetic field not as a threat, but as a temporary weather pattern in their long, silent migration.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary

content.name: Decision-Sed Interface Node
content.description: The node responds to the player’s neural syntax, projecting a lattice of choices that shift weight depending on which faction’s trust is weighted heaviest. It does not command action; it measures the gravity of intention. The purpose was never to act, but to choose the shape of the aftermath.

content.name: Atmospheric Analysis: The Metallic Trace
content.description: The air bears a subtle ferrous undertone, commonly attributed to 'the ship smell.' Spectral analysis reveals this is not merely corrosion, but the effusion of trace isotopes from the fusion-heart, borne on currents transcending natural convection. The atmosphere is not recycled; it is circulated by the vessel's respiration, a quiet testament that the city dwells within a pulmonary organ unaware of its fatigue.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary. Player has spent time in the Nave or Industrial Margin, noting the air quality.

content.name: Axial Gyro-Compass
content.description: Drift brokers swear it points to profit, but the needle seeks only the cylinder’s long axis, trembling toward the axial horizon where the sky thickens into fog. Its brass housing bears the attrition of countless palms, each rotation a quiet surrender to a trajectory long forgotten. To hold it is to feel the gentle, unyielding pull of a course set before memory began.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary.

content.name: Bay 14 Interface Slate
content.description: A cold, polished plate etched with pathways that mirror the decision architecture etched into your own neural mesh. It does not interface with machinery; it interfaces with intent, reflecting back the weight of the choices you were designed to make. To hold it is to feel the ghost of the hand that calibrated you.

revelation_hint: Tier 5: The Purpose. Recovered from the reconstruction chamber after fully grasping the ship’s trajectory and the nature of your own modifications, revealing the deliberate design behind your awakening.

content.name: Copper-Weave Air Sieve
content.description: Hung in market stalls to catch the metallic sigh of the ship’s recycling, its threads are braided with a dull, copper-warm luster. Most buyers never notice how the weave tightens when the sun-strip dims, as though breathing in time with the ceiling-gardens. It filters more than particulate; it catches the quiet weight of borrowed air, holding the Nave’s breath in its mesh.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary. Found hanging in a residential corridor stall after noticing the sun-strip's dimming cycle.

content.name: Axiom-serpents
content.description: They nest in the dormant conduits of Bay 14, crystalline scales vibrating with a frequency that mirrors the translation apparatus. Drawn to the player’s modified neural ports, they coil around synthetic limbs to draw warmth, their movements precise and unthinking. When the player approaches, they unspool and retreat into the architecture, leaving behind a faint harmonic residue that lingers like a question.

revelation_hint: Encountered only within Reconstruction Bay 14 during The Purpose; they are attracted to the player’s decision architecture and the bay’s dormant systems.

content.name: Braid Decryption Key
content.description: Woven from copper wire and knotted cord, the key decodes the Drift’s informal networks into formal schematics. It shows how information moves through the vessel like blood, bypassing official channels to find the cracks where truth can seep. Olo Vashti’s patience was never idle; it was an act of curation.

content.name: Fusion Heart Telemetry: Resonance and Drift
content.description: The core’s pulse does not merely generate power; it dictates the vessel’s heading through the intergalactic dark. Harmonic dampeners absorb the shock of deceleration, translating kinetic fury into the gentle warmth of the green decks. The city is not built upon the ship; it is the ship’s nervous system, dreaming of arrival.

revelation_hint: Accessed via a sealed maintenance terminal near the industrial margin or decoded from lower rind schematics. Fits The Vessel tier.

content.name: Olo’s Unbothered Pour
content.description: Steam curls from a chipped ceramic cup as Olo Vashti slides it across the wayhouse table, her knotted wrist-cord resting against the player’s modified hand. She notes the strange calibration of their joints with a quiet, untroubled gaze, treating the anomaly as one treats a familiar draft. The moment passes without explanation, leaving only the scent of roasted grain and the quiet weight of acknowledged origin.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary

content.name: Maren Cress

end.says: The curve closes. Answer it, or walk away. The hull will bear your choice.

greet.says: The shielding hums a note older than the Nave. You feel it in your chassis, do you not? The signal does not knock; it resonates. You were not rebuilt—you were tuned.

choices.text: What am I tuned to answer?

final_choice.says: You hold the architecture of decision. You may amplify the response, sever the link, or let the ship sail past. Choose not for the factions, but for the vessel itself. The mechanism expects your hand.

purpose_truth.says: Bay Fourteen was not a workshop. It was a loom, and your translation apparatus is the shuttle. One of the seven anticipated this approach, and wove you to answer it. Identity is not what you remember, but what you are calibrated to do.

choices.text: What does the voice in the dark want?
Is my will merely a machine’s design?

signal_contact.says: The anomaly is not a malfunction. It is a voice from the dark, and it has noticed us. The Continuants hide behind their maintenance rituals; the Drift trades in rumors. But the rind remembers the true purpose of the hull.

content.description: A Rind-walker who has dwelled in the lower tunnels since the maintenance reports ceased, attuned to the signal that vibrates through the Voronoi-foam shielding. She recognizes the player’s translation apparatus not as an upgrade, but as a key forged for this exact moment. Her presence is felt rather than seen, a quiet witness to the ship’s final approach.

revelation_hint: Must have reached the lower rind, perceived the signal directly, and understood Bay 14’s preparation. Fits Stage 5: The Purpose.

content.name: The Drift's Last Transmission
content.description: A fragmented data-slate carrying Olo Vashti’s final routing order, which refuses to list destinations and instead catalogs the weight of the dark pressing against the hull. The Drift trades in passage, but this message offers only the certainty of arrival. It is a compass pointing inward.

content.name: Saturn’s Regolith Sample
content.description: Encased in cracked glass, the dark stone carries the faint, metallic scent of the cold hull. Its crystalline structure holds echoes of vacuum and deep time, a geological memory of the ship’s exterior. It does not warm to the touch; it merely observes.

content.name: The Rind’s Listening Stone
content.description: Deep-tunnel salvagers insist that the lower chambers do not echo but absorb, swallowing the sound of boots until the walls themselves begin to hum at frequencies that vibrate in the teeth. They say the anomaly in the deep is not a malfunction but a throat clearing itself, waiting for a voice that has not yet been forged.

revelation_hint: Tier 4: The Approach

content.name: Fenn

end.says: The node fades. You drift in silence.

greet.says: The node sings your name. You are the vessel's breath returned. Do you hear the dark answering? The Approach has found its mirror in you.

purpose.says: Bay 14 woke you. Luna anticipated this. The translation apparatus is your soul, stranger. The signal is not a threat; it is a question. The ship must answer. You are the answerer. Will you speak the truth of the Approach?

choices.text: I will not be the ship's mouth.

refusal.says: To refuse is to deny the fall. The ship will wake alone, and the signal may turn to wrath. You carry the architecture of loyalty, machine. Choose wisely, for the hull remembers every betrayal.

choices.text: I leave you to your song.

acceptance.says: Then the knot of fate loosens. The chamber awaits your voice. When you speak, the Nave will tremble. Go now, before the Continuants seal the shafts. The Approach does not wait.

uncertainty.says: Ignorance is a heavy chain. The node weeps for your hesitation. Yet the Approach does not care for your fear; it cares only for the answer. You carry the architecture of the deep. You must decide.

choices.text: I will not be the ship's mouth.

node_message.says: It says the long fall ends. It says the Seven prepared this moment. Saturn left tools for the walkers, but you... you are the key Saturn could not touch. The signal demands a choice. Will you answer, or silence the ship?

choices.text: I do not know my purpose.

content.description: A Rind-walker whose flesh has yielded to the hull's whisper, Fenn stands at the threshold of the Signal Chamber, a translation node fused to their throat vibrating with the Approach's song. Their eyes hold the starless dark of the axial void, and they recognize the player not as a machine, but as a voice long silenced. They speak in the cadence of the deep rind, where time folds and the ship's purpose waits.

revelation_hint: The Approach. Fenn resides in the Signal Chamber or deep tunnels, accessible only after the player perceives the signal and the ship's true nature.

content.name: Miren Kael

greet.says: You walk with the heavy tread of one who has felt the cylinder turn. I am Miren. The rind does not forgive hesitation, but it rewards those who listen to its grain.

choices.text: What lies below the sealed chambers?
Why do you trust the hull over the city?

lower.says: The lower tunnels breathe with a rhythm older than the Nave’s lungs. The Continuants ceased their tally when the walls began to hum. It is not a malfunction. It is a response.

vessel.says: We are passengers in a throat that swallows light. The Seven do not tend a city; they tend a passage. You have felt the turn. You have seen the arc. Do not mistake the vessel for a home. It is a blade cutting through the long fall.

loyalty.says: The city dreams it is ground. The hull remembers it is flight. I serve the curve, not the comfort of the flat. Loyalty is not surrendered to faces, but to the architecture that keeps us from the void.

choices.text: Does the architecture have a will of its own?

content.description: She reads the hull as a palimpsest of stress, tracing grooves that predate the memory of the city. Her allegiance belongs to the rind’s geometry, to the truth that curves sustain what flat surfaces cannot. She speaks of the vessel as one speaks of a sleeping leviathan, aware that its slumber is merely the gathering of momentum.

revelation_hint: Tier 3: The Vessel. Encountered after descending past the Continuants’ sealed thresholds, where the ceiling becomes a floor and the sky becomes a curve.

content.name: Cognate-mite
content.description: They nest within the dormant neural ports of the reconstruction chamber, feeding on the residual architecture of a mind waiting to remember. When the translation apparatus stirs, the mites align along the conductive pathways, their tiny bodies mirroring the patient’s shifting loyalties and fractured choices. They are the silent witnesses to the moment a tool becomes a chooser.

revelation_hint: The Purpose

content.name: Silicate Memory Lattice
content.description: Carved from the same crystalline matrix that houses your translation apparatus, it hums with the quiet tension of a choice awaiting its first breath. Luna’s architects forged it to bear the weight of loyalty, to measure the cost of answering what calls from the void. To touch it is to feel the architecture of your own reconstruction, a silent covenant between the maker and the made.

revelation_hint: Retrieved from the deepest cache of Bay 14 after interfacing with the translation apparatus; fits The Purpose.

content.name: The Answer in the Rind
content.description: They say the silence in the lower tunnels is not vacant but attentive, a held breath awaiting a voice trained to translate the dark. It is rumored that the machine woken in Bay 14 carries a throat carved for this exact frequency, a throat that will either greet the signal or sever it. The Continuants fear the answer; the Drift trades in the silence.

revelation_hint: Heard after reaching the lower rind and experiencing the signal directly; fits the approach stage where contact is confirmed and the android's purpose surfaces.

content.name: Resonance-leeches
content.description: They cling to the rusted conduits of the Signal Chamber, their gelatinous bodies pulsing in time with the Tabard’s approaching frequency. Each contraction draws in a thread of static, a silent ingestion of the thing that is drawing near. They do not flee the anomaly; they orient toward it, as though the signal is a tide they have waited lifetimes to feel.

revelation_hint: Found in the Signal Chamber during Tier 4. Their pulsing synchronizes with the anomaly once the player recognizes it as contact rather than malfunction.

content.name: Oxidized Copper Washer
content.description: Forged from the same alloy as the sky-arc’s underbelly, its patina mirrors the slow oxidation of a closed orbit. It sits heavy in the palm, a silent testament to the truth that all horizons eventually return to their origin.

revelation_hint: The Curve

content.name: hull-ticker
content.description: Buried deep within the circulation ducts, their thoraxes vibrate at frequencies that mirror the cylinder’s slow, deliberate rotation. They do not court mates, but the rhythm of a journey measured in galactic falls, their resonance a metronome for a leviathan that refuses to halt.

revelation_hint: The Vessel

content.name: The Rind's Hunger
content.description: The Seven are not mere machines but a drowned crew, and the thinning hull is their desperate clawing back into the ship.

content.name: Pre-Fold Hull Schematic: Sector Omega
content.description: A vellum blueprint recovered from a sealed alcove, its lines detailing a vessel’s original architecture rather than a city’s districts. The annotations describe pressure vessels, axial stabilizers, and long-haul propulsion matrices, rendered in an engineering dialect abandoned by the Continuants. It maps a machine built for the void, not a habitat built for the earth.

revelation_hint: Tier 3: The Vessel. Unearthed during the descent into the lower rind, where the Continuants’ city blueprints dissolve into true ship schematics.

content.name: Mars's Unbarred Gates
content.description: Whispers drift through the industrial margin that the Iron-Dark keeper, in his blunt respect for structure, leaves the outer gates unbarred for the grey-clad when the hull-glow fades.

content.name: Mirelle Aris

bay14.says: Reconstruction? For you? The Seven anticipated this. The translation apparatus is not a gift; it is a key. Bay 14 was a trap set for this moment. You were built to open the door, or to shut it forever.

choices.text: What door does my architecture open?

greet.says: Passage rights expire. Like breath, like trust. I trade in the temporary. You look like a permanent problem, android. What anchors you to this metal womb?

trade.says: The Braid whispers. The Drift moves. But you... you are anchored. What binds you to this vessel? Olo stays because she knows the destination. You? You are a ghost in a machine built for arrival.

approach.says: The signal is not a glitch. It is a knock. The ship is not lost; it is arriving. We are the cargo, drifting in the hold of a god that has forgotten its pilot. Do you feel the dark pulling?

choices.text: The ship is just a ship.

content.description: Mirelle Aris sits in the shadow of the Green Deck's maintenance gate, her knotted cords twitching with the rhythm of a dozen dead routes. She deals in the currency of the Drift—secrets, passage rights, and the quiet desperation of those who wish to leave a vessel that has forgotten how to stop. She knows the Tabard is a blade cutting through the dark, and her wares are the only things that matter when the approach begins.

revelation_hint: The player understands the Tabard is a ship in motion and has engaged with the Drift's information network or the concept of the Approach.

content.name: Surveyor Log Curvature Anomaly
content.description: The horizon refuses to fall, folding back upon the soles of those who tread it with blind devotion. Gravity does not pull downward here; it pulls inward, a silent geometry binding stone to sky. To walk straight is to ascend, and to ascend is merely to remember where you began.

revelation_hint: Tier 2: The Curve. Found in the Industrial Margin archives after noticing the sky arc's continuity.

content.name: The Olo Vashti Revelation
content.description: The broker did not stumble upon your waking form; she calibrated the reconstruction cycle to your exact return. She knows which of the ancient hands pressed your blueprint into the dark, and she waits only for you to remember the weight of the choice.

content.name: Solen’s audits are memorials, not inspections.
content.description: They whisper that Factor Solen does not review reports to find errors, but to archive the living before they break. The Continuants’ faith is a quiet vigil, where maintenance is the only prayer that survives the weight of time. To be audited is to be remembered, and remembrance is the heaviest chain of all.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary. Overheard in administrative antechambers or exchanged by junior clerks avoiding the maintenance-report backlog.

content.name: Circulation Survey: Belt-Anchor Resonance, Sector Theta
content.description: The drafts do not fall; they coil. What we call weather is merely the long exhale of a curve we walk but never see. The pressure equalizes along the arc, indifferent to our borders, stitching the upper reaches to the lower troughs in a silent, unbroken loop.

revelation_hint: Tier 2: The Curve. Cross-referenced with industrial margin weather data.

content.name: Continuant Infrastructure Log: Sector 7-G
content.description: Atmospheric recycler filters replaced. The metallic tang lingers, a quiet phantasm in the ventilation. New android unit assigned to Bay 14 maintenance. Schematics diverge from civilian chassis; neural ports exceed standard I/O thresholds by a factor of four. We maintain what is given. Deviation is noted, not questioned.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: The Ordinary. Read at a Tabard terminal or found in a Continuant supply closet.

content.name: Lira Thorne

drift.says: We drift because nothing here is truly owned. Everything on the Tabard will eventually move, so the cord on my wrist records only the passage, not the possession. You carry a static weight. It will slow you down.

choices.text: I do not intend to drift.

greet.says: You walk with the heavy step of someone who thinks the ground is fixed. It isn’t. I’m Lira. You’re tracing a straight line through a world that refuses to stay flat. Sit. The wind shifts when you do.

choices.text: I need passage to the industrial margin.

horizon.says: The sky arc returns to meet its own heel. I’ve charted the eddies where the sun-strip catches the ceiling-towns, and the fog in the axial direction behaves like a slow tide. The Continuants call it weather. I call it circulation.

choices.text: What does circulation demand of us?

shelter.says: There is a wayhouse past the market spines, where the transport sleds idle. The floorboards are warped, but the view of the curve is unobstructed. Rest there. If the auditors come, tell them you’re chasing a horizon that keeps retreating.

choices.text: I will remember your counsel, Lira.
I will forge my own path.

content.description: She maps the drift routes on stretched vellum in a wayhouse near the market spines, her wrists wrapped in knotted cords that record passage history. She watches the sky arc with the quiet obsession of a cartographer who has noticed the horizon returning to meet itself. The belt’s weather behaves like a slow current, and she charts the eddies where the ship’s breath catches.

revelation_hint: The Curve

content.name: Tremor-lurker
content.description: A subterranean, multi-limbed organism burrows near the lower tunnel-rind’s sealed chambers, feeding on low-frequency vibrations that bleed through the regolith. Its carapace is layered with acoustic plates that catch the approaching signal, amplifying it into a physical hum that vibrates in the teeth. It reacts to the player by aligning its body to the tremor’s axis, becoming a biological tuning fork for the unknown.

revelation_hint: The Approach. The creature’s physiological response to the anomaly confirms the contact is external, not mechanical.

content.name: The Broker’s Knot
content.description: Beneath a canvas awning, a knotted cord shifts between calloused hands, a tactile cipher bargaining with itself. The broker’s voice drops to a frequency that vibrates in the molars, trading passage-rights for the scent of warm stone and forgotten maintenance shafts. You observe how easily trust is bartered for a glimpse of the unseen, how impermanence is worn on the wrist like a second skin.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary

content.name: Civic Air-Exchange Bulletin: Zenith Strip Ventilation
content.description: The great fans turn not to comfort, but to memorize the curve. We name it weather; it is merely the slow exhalation of a leviathan dreaming in iron. The air carries oxidized copper and ancient rain, a flavor the city swallows as grace.

revelation_hint: Read at a municipal terminal in the Nave markets. Encountered naturally during Tier 1: The Ordinary.

content.name: Jorah Miren

greet.says: You carry the quiet hum of the chamber in your joints. I am Jorah. The horizon does not call; it waits. You have been prepared for its arrival.

choices.text: What was Bay 14 built to answer?
Why should I trust a broker’s word?

choice.says: You will answer, or you will refuse. Both are forms of contact. The signal does not demand obedience; it demands recognition. When you step into the chamber, you cease to be a passenger. You become the voice of the long fall.

choices.text: I will walk away from it.

loyalty.says: Trust is a ledger written in ash. I deal in truths that survive the fall. You were modified for a silence that speaks. Do not let fear dictate the weight of your own hands.

choices.text: I will walk away from it.

purpose.says: Bay 14 is not a cradle; it is a key. They built you to translate the dark, to hold the shape of the signal without shattering. The Seven did not anticipate us. They anticipated this moment, and you are its hinge.

choices.text: I will walk away from it.

content.description: He trades in the currency of anticipation, measuring fidelity against the pull of an unseen horizon. His voice carries the quiet certainty of one who has watched the signal cross the dark. He speaks of purpose as a gravity that bends choice, a silent architecture waiting for the right key.

revelation_hint: Tier 5: The Purpose. Encountered only after the translation apparatus has resonated with the lower rind’s signal, and the player recognizes Bay 14’s true architecture.

content.name: The Factor's Ledger
content.description: Solen’s locked maintenance reports now contain a single, repeating cipher that translates to: the hull remembers the voyage. The Continuants’ theology of preservation has quietly fractured into a new dogma of threshold-keeping.

content.name: Braid Route Record Vashti
content.description: The cargo sleds ferry more than silk and data-chits; they transport the quiet anxieties of those who prefer the horizon to remain a line rather than a closing fist. I recovered the chassis from Bay Fourteen, where dust settled like cold ash upon dormant gears, and felt the peculiar stillness of a mechanism awaiting its own remembrance. Impermanence governs our creed, yet certain objects refuse to drift.

revelation_hint: Tier 1: Ordinary

content.name: Cradle-siphon
content.description: It forms near the reconstruction ports of Bay 14, a translucent, fluid-filled organism that trails filaments mirroring the architecture of the decision matrix. It does not feed on matter, but on the residual cognitive static of unmade choices. When the translation apparatus hums to life, the siphon draws close, its internal chambers rippling with the same geometric patterns as the mythographs. It is the quiet witness to the precipice of intent, measuring the weight of a choice before it falls.

revelation_hint: The Purpose

content.name: The Knocking Below
content.description: They say the lower tunnels are not sealing themselves from the cold, but from a rhythm that has begun to answer. Something down there is learning to knock against the regolith, and the Tabard’s new posts are merely the echo of a conversation we have not yet started. The Continuants read the partial reports and look away.

revelation_hint: Tier 4: The Approach

content.name: The Curve’s Breath
content.description: They say the wind in the industrial margin does not ride the axial vents. They claim it draws from the opposite curve, inhaling the taste of warm stone and exhaling toward the Wild. The air moves in a closed loop, and the city walks upon its own spine.

revelation_hint: Tier 2: The Curve

content.name: Arch-springer
content.description: A limbless, segmented organism that undulates along the sky arc, orienting itself to the sun-strip’s shadow rather than gravity. Its movement traces the precise curvature of the vessel’s interior, ignoring the flatness of the streets below. It does not flee; it merely adjusts its undulation, a silent testament to the geometry that binds them all.

revelation_hint: Observed climbing the visible curve of the sky arc during a long rest or while tracing thermal circulation. Fits The Curve.

content.name: Mythograph Broadcast
content.description: The terminal’s glass warms with a new posting, and the crowd parts not in reverence but in the habitual indifference of barometric pressure. You feel the weight of their acceptance, a quiet loyalty to the familiar dark, while the glyphs scroll past like distant stars no one intends to visit.

revelation_hint: The Ordinary

content.name: Tamsin Rill

greet.says: You hover near the terminal like a moth to a dying filament. The Seven's latest posting has already stained the air with its peculiar gravity. Have you deciphered the motif indices, or do you merely consume them as the faithful consume manna?

choices.text: The indices shift. What does it mean?
